characters.name: Akimoto
characters.description: Akimoto(秋本,Akimoto)is a minor character of theFruits Basketseries. She was a member of the all-woman gang named "The Ladies", the same gangArisa Uotaniwas a part of in middle school. While she did indulge in criminal activities and was a member of a violent gang, Akimoto appeared to be more sympathetic and considerate compared to her fellow gang-members. It is implied that like Arisa, Akimoto had always wanted to leave the gang and start her life anew, and did so after indirectly saving Arisa.[1][2]

characters.name: Akira Sohma
characters.description: Akira was described as a beautifully kind person. However, he was also very melancholy because those around him did not understand his loneliness and only treated him as the head of the Sohma family. Ren was the first person to recognize his loneliness and fear of dying alone, and as such, he fell in love with her. Although he had an earnest love for Akito, it is hinted that his parenting may have, in fact, been misguided. By repeatedly telling Akito that she was God and thus destined to unconditional love, he would ultimately cause Akito to develop her incredibly warped view that every member of the zodiac had to love her, despite her whims and repeated acts of either physical and/or emotional abuse, along with her emotional immaturity in recognizing the reasons why her actions were wrong in the first place. Therefore, it can be assumed that Akira may have spoiled Akito excessively and had not taught her the difference between right and wrong, despite his otherwise good intentions. Although this may have been somewhat justified, given Akito's short age at the time and his possible need to be a more doting parental figure due to Ren's more-than-evident hostility, his failure to properly discipline his daughter ultimately left him unable to be a proper parent for Akito.

characters.name: Akito Sohma
characters.description: Aside from her occasional bad temper tantrums when she was young, Akito was a gentle, pampered, and sweet little girl. Due to her father spoiling her, Akito grew up believing that she was born to be loved by everyone and that everyone must do as she says. On the other hand, her mother's psychological and emotional abuse led Akito to be desperate for displays of affection, and she would go around the household asking everyone if they loved her. However, after Akira's death, Kureno's curse breaking, and as a product of Ren's psychological and emotional abuse, including being forcefully raised as a boy and being poisoned with hatred and insecurities, Akito snapped and changed to a mentally and emotionally unstable person whose kindness warped into insanity and cruelty.[3][4] Even though Akito appears as a frail, mysterious, and composed woman at first glance, Akito has horribly abused many of the Zodiac members to take out her anger over her father's death and her mother’s insistence that she is unneeded, worthless and that no one will ever truly love her. She is shown to be an incredibly arrogant, manipulative, selfish, immature and cruel young woman who rarely shows any sympathy to others and can be calm one moment and having a screaming tantrum the next. She is mostly feared and respected by many members of the Sohma clan. Akito also insists the male Zodiac members are obligated to only love her because she believes the Zodiac animals should direct all their affection to their God. So whenever one of them directs their attention to anyone other than her, Akito is overcome with fear that she's going to be abandoned like her mother predicted she would. Moreover, Akito is extremely misogynistic despite being a woman herself, since she projects her own mother onto every woman she meets. As a result, she gets very jealous of women who "steals" the male Zodiacs' attention. Notable examples are when she threwRinout of a window because she was datingHatsuharu,[5]beatKisaup badly becauseHiroconfessed his love with her,[6]and guilt-trippingKanafor wanting to marryHatoriby practically blinding him in one eye.[7]Despite this, Akito cannot fathom being to blame for anything and justifies her controlling and abusive treatment by claiming that a God can do whatever they please and no one can punish them for it. However, behind her intimidating and cruel personality, Akito is incredibly insecure and terrified of being abandoned and unloved. Because she had it instilled into her mind that she was “God” and that her bonds with the Zodiacs defined her entire existence, she doesn’t think she has any self-worth beyond that said title. She desperately clings onto her position as the “God” only because Ren convinced her she is worthless and will never be truly loved by anyone, and to enforce her belief that she will never be alone if she keeps all of them with her, even if she has to resorts to physical and mental torture to make everyone too afraid of her to leave her

characters.name: Amane
characters.description: Amane(あまね,Amane)is a minor character ofFruits Basket Another. She is a student of class 1-A― the same class asSawa Mitoma― atKaibara Municipal High School. She eventually becomes one of Sawa's first genuine friends along withMito. Amane is a fair-skinned teenage girl standing at an average build and height. She has straight, nape-length black hair with long bangs mainly draping over the left side of her face. Additionally, she has several cowlicks sticking out from her hair. She has big light-colored eyes and is often seen with a bright smile. She is often depicted in the typical Kaibara High school uniform with a blue sweater on top. Amane is a very kind-hearted and gentle girl who always thinks of others. For example, she had been worrying about Sawa ever since the entrance ceremony and has been wanting to befriend her― without any ulterior motives.[1]After becoming friends with Sawa, she is shown to be a very cheerful, yet slightly innocent and spacey girl who is always wearing a smile on her lips. Additionally, she is welcoming and supportive of her friends, and very openly expresses her appreciation and affection for them.[2] Amane tends to overthink. She is also easily flustered and can have trouble being level-headed in situations where she feels embarrassed.[1]However, she can also be intuitive, and can read between the lines and sense when people are dishonest with their words and feelings.[2]

characters.name: Arisa Uotani
characters.description: As a young, troubled teenager, Arisa used to be very grumpy and violent, and developed anger issues from a young age. She hated the terms "peaceful" and "kind", to such an extent that the happy home life of Kyoko and Tohru sickened her because she herself had been abandoned by her mother and neglected by her father.[1][2]However, after Kyoko and Tohru helped her turn her life around and quit her gang, Arisa went through a significant change which made her the person she is today.[3] Arisa is now a fun-loving, cool, tough, and stand-offish former gang-member who always tries to get some fun out of something, even when things seem to get rough. While she can be short-tempered and aggressive, traits she shares with Kyo, she is surprisingly very emotional and cries easily about things most people wouldn't; such as Tohru showing up in her old swimsuit from middle school (despite it being a matter of practicality), and again when she thinks about old people buying their meals at a convenience store (even though she admits they must think nothing of it).[1]According to Kureno, Arisa is a very honest and expressive girl who wears her emotions on the sleeve; she cries when she's sad, laughs when she's happy, and yells when she's mad.[4] While she is occasionally aloof and emotionally closed off, she is warm, friendly, and deeply loyal towards her friends and loved ones. Arisa also acts like a big sister figure, and while she has moved on from her past, she never forgot what it taught her. She uses her own experience being a gang-member to help other people, such as lecturing three middle school-agedyankee wannabes. This proved to be effective, as they began respecting Arisa and even began calling her "Ane-sama".[3]On the other hand, she is frigid and hostile towards anyone that gets on her nerves, primarily thePrince Yuki Fan Club, and even occasionally brings out a metal pipe whenever she feels the need to protect Tohru.[5] Arisa is noted to be very direct, tomboyish, as she even the pronoun "boku" (which is normally a masculine pronoun in Japanese). She is brash, headstrong, never beats around the bush, and isn't easily shamed. For instance, she casually says things the other characters become embarrassed at; such as when Kyo yells at her for mentioning underwear when they were shopping for a swimsuit for Tohru.[1]

characters.name: Arisa's Father
characters.description: He is the father ofArisa Uotani. In the past, he constantly spent time in his house, excessively drinking alcohol and watching TV all day, all while neglecting his daughter’s needs.[1]However, when Arisa turned her life around, so did her father; thus, he is now seen to be a fun-loving father and a parent who worries about his daughter’s future.[2]

characters.name: Ayame and Yuki's Mother
characters.description: Ayame and Yuki's mother is a self-centered, arrogant, prideful and selfish woman who pursues materialistic gain at all costs, even at the expense of her sons as she effectively sold Yuki to Akito (due to him being the rat and supposedly the closest to God) so she could have a good life. When confronted about this, she shows absolutely no remorse for the fact she very much used her own son as a tool for personal benefit, and chastising Yuki for complaining despite, in her view, that the family head "preferred" him, either oblivious to the fact Akito inflicted tormentous physical and emotional abuse on Yuki, or simply ignoring for mere convenience (the latter being the most likely, as she was seen ignoring his pleas), although her interactions with Yuki suggest more that she is angry about Yuki not embracing his "role" as the "favourite" and the supposed status that would bring him and further scorning him for his "bad choices". This controlling behaviour is also likely the main reason why she is unnerved by Ayame who is fundamentally a free soul who doesn't fall in line with her selfish wishes. Ayame in turn has stated that their mother is deeply drunk into status and power. She is seen frequently complaining about the hardships of her life, the details of which are never explained but that may well explain why she clings so much to material benefit as a form of happiness, even if it means sacrificing emotional health in the process. Because of her hard rooted beliefs, she often gets unjustifiably angry when defied and can at times dish out very hurtful remarks like believing it was better her sons wouldn't been born so as to not be so burdened. Ayame remarks that she has trampled on the things important to her, suggesting that deep down, she does to some degree care for her sons, but is too absorbed on her views to show proper affection.

Because of her hard rooted beliefs, she often gets unjustifiably angry when defied and can at times dish out very hurtful remarks like believing it was better her sons wouldn't been born so as to not be so burdened. When confronted about this, she shows absolutely no remorse for the fact she very much used her own son as a tool for personal benefit, and chastising Yuki for complaining despite, in her view, that the family head "preferred" him, either oblivious to the fact Akito inflicted tormentous physical and emotional abuse on Yuki, or simply ignoring for mere convenience (the latter being the most likely, as she was seen ignoring his pleas), although her interactions with Yuki suggest more that she is angry about Yuki not embracing his "role" as the "favourite" and the supposed status that would bring him and further scorning him for his "bad choices". characters.name: Ayame Sohma
characters.description: He is extremely flamboyant and overconfident, often speaking of himself in a much brighter light than others, while in truth he just wishes that he and Yuki were closer. One of the few things as brothers that Yuki and Ayame have in common, is that they both enjoy annoying Kyo. A few of the names that Ayame calls Kyo are "Lucky Kyou" and "Kyon-Kichi", both of which he calls Kyo to annoy him, which he succeeds at doing. Ayame is constantly trying to get closer to Yuki due to their separation as children. He once told Tohru that he "was vaguely aware that he even had a brother. By the time he realized it, a deep 'rift' had grown between [Yuki and Ayame]".[2]Yuki also once admitted to Tohru, "I wouldn't say I dislike [Ayame]. He just gets to be too much for me sometimes, is all". Ayame and Shigure pretend to be a couple and often pretend to do little bromance' scenes where at the end they say 'All right!' and flash a thumbs-up, much to the dismay of Hatori, Kyo, Yuki, and the confusion of Tohru. He also tends to be a bit of a pervert. For example, when Tohru and Yuki visited his shop, he said any dream could be completed, such as the dream of seeing a woman stepping out of a bathroom. The result of that was Yuki shouting, "That is a crime!"

characters.name: Chiaki Hasada
characters.description: Chiaki Hasada(羽佐田 千明,Hasada Chiaki)is a minor character ofFruits Basket Another. He serves as the secretary duringHajime Sohma's presidency. Chiaki is a teenage boy who is rather tall for his age. He has a light complexion and is often seen with a neutral face. He has sharp, dark-colored eyes, and light-colored, slightly spiked and cropped hair. His bangs mainly drape over the right side of his face. Although Chiaki often holds a neutral or blank face, he is a friendly young man who gets along well with people.[1]He values teamwork, and is often seen relying on his student council members as much as they rely on him. Chiaki is one of the most responsible members of the student council and is very rarely seen slacking off. Because of this, he often looks out for his fellow student council members. While he certainly is tired ofHajime SohmaandMutsuki Sohma's bickering and Mutsuki's tendency to slack off, he does not press on it.[1] Although Chiaki often holds a neutral or blank face, he is a friendly young man who gets along well with people.[1]He values teamwork, and is often seen relying on his student council members as much as they rely on him. Chiaki is one of the most responsible members of the student council and is very rarely seen slacking off. Because of this, he often looks out for his fellow student council members. While he certainly is tired ofHajime SohmaandMutsuki Sohma's bickering and Mutsuki's tendency to slack off, he does not press on it.[1] Chiaki is close to his student council members as well as the other Sohmas, evidenced by the fact that he had gone to the Sohma vacation house before, as well as by the fact that he was invited toMichi Manabe'ssukiyakiparty.[2]Additionally, Chiaki can have a fair amount of fun too, though his humor is mostly deadpan.[1][3]

characters.name: Chizuru Sohma
characters.description: Chizuru Sohma(草摩 千弦,Sōma Chizuru)is one of the recurruing characters ofFruits Basket Another. He is the son ofMineandAyame Sohma, and the younger brother ofHibika Sohma. He is foul-mouthed, but what he says has wisdom. He is currently a second-year middle school student and a member of the Go Club along with his friendsShiki SohmaandRio Mosca. Chizuru is a young teenage boy of average build and height. Overall, he takes after his father and hispaternal uncle, having the same facial features as his father and similar hair- and eye color as his uncle. He has short, slightly spiked and fluffy, straight dark-grey hair with long, messy bangs framing his face, with an additional cowlick sticking up from the top of his head. He has light grey eyes, and is often seen with a frown or annoyed face. Chizuru has a hot temper and frequently lashes out at other people. He can also be very aggressive, foul-mouthed, and rash; such as acting before thinking, and having no problem with telling people off when they annoy him. He acts very nonchalantly and is left rather dumbfounded by his sister's antics. On the other hand, he is not against some light teasing. Chizuru describes himself as an extremely normal child compared to his family and admits that he is nothing like either of his parents. He also gets tired of all the comments customers make that he is so normal compared to his father’s weirdness. He vaguely mentions that he feels like he is the odd one out in the family, andMichi Manabementions that Chizuru is going through his rebellious phase, which may explain his occasional rude behavior. Despite that, Chizuru is very level-headed, responsible, and down to earth. According toSawa Mitoma, he has good manners, a good head on his shoulders, and is good at looking after people. She also mentions that despite his harsh words, the edges of his words come off as kind and loving. Additionally, when he has calmed down, been able to reflect on his actions, and analyze the situation he has been put in, Chizuru has no problem apologizing for his actions.[1]

characters.name: God of The Chinese Zodiac
characters.description: The firstGod of The Chinese Zodiacwas once upon a time a very lonely person who became friends with thirteen animals who regarded them as a "god". Not wanting to part with their friends, the god cast a spell that would bind them and the animals in an everlasting bond throughout their next lives. This is the origin of theSohma family curse. They were a very lonely person who chose to isolate themselves due to having "the strength of a thousand men", "the lives of a thousand men", and "a thousand memories". Since they knew they were different from everyone else, they were afraid of themselves, other people, and getting hurt. Because of this, they chose to make friends with animals instead. Through this decision, it is revealed that they were a very gentle and kind person who valued their friends deeply. Their love towards their friends and their fear of being lonely, the person created a spell so they and their friends could stay connected for eternity even after death. They never intended for this bond to become the source of generations of abuse in the Sohma linage.[1]

characters.name: Hajime Sohma
characters.description: Kazuma Sohma(Adoptive Paternal Grandfather)Unnamed Parental GrandfatherUnnamed Parental Grandmother†Katsuya Honda(Maternal Grandfather)†Kyoko Honda(Maternal Grandmother)†Unnamed Maternal Great-grandfatherUnnamed Maternal Great-grandmother†Unnamed Great-AuntUnnamed First cousin once removedUnnamed First cousin once removedUnnamed Maternal Great-grandfatherUnnamed Maternal Great-grandmotherUnnamed Sister-in-lawUnnamed Niece —Hajime Sohma Hajime Sohma(草摩 はじめ,Sōma Hajime)is one of the deuteragonists of theFruits Basket Anothermanga series. He is the son ofTohruandKyo Sohmaand is the oldest child of three siblings. Hajime initially comes off like a slightly closed off and easily annoyed person with a hot temper, and is usually uninterested in what's going on around him. He has a sharp tongue, is very blunt and has a tendency to say the first thing on his mind, only realizing the (potential hurtful) things he said just a moment later. These qualities of him are you often seen in his conversations with Mutsuki and when he is upset. However, otherwise, Hajime is very laid-back, easy-going, and easy to get along with. Due to his position as the student council president, he is very level-headed, responsible and ends up helping people even if he doesn't want to. Hajime is also noted to be wise and have an older-brother type of personality due to growing up with two younger siblings. He is understanding of other people's feelings and tends to give parental lectures, such as tellingSawa Mitomathat it's okay to rely on people and that not being picky is good; telling Mutsuki that he needs to clean his student council desk and be more responsible; as well scoldingMina Sohmafor running around just after recovering from a fever. Due to these tendencies, Hajime is called "Dad" by his relatives; much to his chagrin. He also has a soft side, and like Tohru, he is nurturing, self-sufficient and good at housework, including cooking and cleaning. Like Kyo, he can get easily embarrassed and run away from awkward situations if he feels like it's too much for him to handle.

characters.name: Hatori Sohma
characters.description: Hatori is a quiet and serious man who is composed at all times. At first glance, he is mentioned to be rather cold and intimidating, which is why people can misunderstand him. He has also resigned himself to the fact that the Zodiacs should not love or be loved. However, behind his somber and stern personality, Hatori is deeply kind and compassionate, and puts others' happiness before his own. Ayame mentions that his kindness is his best trait as it is his worst. This is especially evident by the fact that Hatori is genuinely happy that Kana could find happiness with another man even after forgetting him. He is also genuinely upset at his inability to protect the younger Zodiacs from Akito's wrath, and decides to watch overTohru Hondaand the younger Zodiacs from making the same mistakes he made.[2]Moreover, the reason why Hatori did not want Tohru to involve herself with the Sohmas is because he did not want her to suffer the same heartbreak as Kana did.[3] Hatori has a very low opinion of himself because of the people he hurt with his memory-surpressing powers. characters.name: Hatsuharu Sohma
characters.description: Haru has two personalities, black and white (also known as dark and cheerful). Under normal circumstances (when he is "white"), Hatsuharu is laid-back and kind and usually has a plain, straight-faced look, taking everything very calmly and is usually more quiet and respectful. When he turns "black", however, he becomes violent, perverted, short-tempered, rude, foul-mouthed, loud and cruel. Despite this, he is an older-brother figure to the younger zodiac members and is immensely loyal to the ones he cares about, particularlyYuki Sohma,Rin Sohma,Kisa Sohma,Hiro Sohmaand to an extentTohru Hondaas well. Although younger than Yuki or Kyo he displays a maturity beyond his age and is shown to be observant, he can also be a prankster at times, although he means well. Best seen when he whispered into Tohru's ear which looked suggestive but in truth it was for her to call Yuki by his name, which embarrassed both Tohru and Yuki when it happened to the point the latter transformed, but was nonetheless immensely happy. He becomes Black if provoked (For example: In the episode "Make it Clear If It's Black or White", Haru goes Black because of Kyo refusing to fight him). Haru will also turn into a full chaotic "black" when something major happens to him such as Rin leaving him. Haru also bottles up his emotions and tries to help others without thinking of himself, usually causing a mental breakdown. It is eventually disclosed that his "Black Haru" personality was born as a defense mechanism in order to cope with the many insults and mockery he received as a youngster.

characters.name: Hinata Sohma
characters.description: InFruits Basket Another, Hinata appears as a kind, caring, outgoing, and energetic woman. However, she is also noted to be carefree and somewhat ditzy and childish. Despite that, she is dependable and responsible, as she was entrusted to take care ofShiki Sohmaduring their trip to the Sohma vacation house. She takes her role as one of the oldest Sohmas seriously, and will go the extra mile for the younger children.[1]

characters.name: Hiro Sohma
characters.description: Hiro is prickly, easily riled up, and exceedingly good at making sarcastic comments and complaining, as he is very prone to insulting people and delivering snarky remarks.[3][4]He enjoys quibbling and can talk circles around most adults. He uses pretentiously large words and can keep arguing without even pausing for breath, until people like Tohru can't keep up.[2]He is hypocritical to a certain degree as well; while he can't stand people who complain all the time, he does a fair share of complaining too. He also points out that Tohru is too clingy with her mother when it turns out he's just as fond of his own mother. Hiro is a very serious and level-headed boy, though this doesn't keep his mouth from getting him in trouble or give him the emotional maturity to handle difficult situations. He was initially seen being very rude towards Tohru, and made putdowns about her weaknesses. He also lashes out against Tohru for her close friendship with Kisa because she comforted her when he couldn't. While he often sounds accusing and often makes people dislike him unnecessarily, he realizes that he should stop talking so harshly, and hates himself for picking trivial fights. However, he can't quite apologize and has such trouble expressing his feelings honestly that sometimes argumentative things are all that come out.[2]At the same time, Hiro is extremely kind towards Kisa but has a "tsundere" personality around her, as although he loves her more than anything, he will get very shy, timid, and has trouble expressing himself around her. Despite his arrogant personality and acting like he's above everyone else, he feels horrible about not being able to help Kisa andIsuzu Sohmawhen they needed help the most. Since he had to keep quiet about Isuzu's incident and the truth about her injuries, it weighs heavily on him. And he breaks down in tears when he can't hold back anymore and explains the truth toHatsuharu Sohma.[5] Throughout the series, while he remains the person he is at heart, Hiro is actively trying to change for the better, both for his own sake and for Kisa's sake. He doesn't like the childish part of him that lashes out at other people, and wants to quickly mature into a composed adult who is able to protect the people he cares about. Hiro's personality was half-stemmed from his mother's ditzy and clumsy persona saying that one day he looked at her and thought that he had to be strong. When his younger sister Hinata is born, Hiro undergoes another change; he becomes a loving and doting older brother, but also more self-aware and even quite philosophical.[5]Hiro gradually mellows down to a much gentler and considerate boy, who is always ready to help others out while in distress.[6]

characters.name: Isuzu Sohma
characters.description: Prior to the start of Fruits Basket, Rin was noted to be a sweet and kind girl at a young age. Although it's worth noting that she had a relatively happy home growing up, things eventually began to take a turn when she began to suffer from a multitude of abuse and abandonment from her parents, constantly. As such, her self-worth and trust in people (aside from Haru, the latter of which she became close with growing up) were destroyed badly. Despite blaming herself for her own misfortunes, followed by the circumstances of believing that her parents wouldn’t have hated and abandoned her if she wasn't viewed as a “bad” child in their eyes, she grew majorly depressed, as a result. And while her parents would never show her an ounce of love or kindness, it's thanks to Haru, that Rin was able to retain her sweet and kind personality as before. At the point when Rin and Haru began a relationship, and that she decided to come clean to Akito about it, as a means of having to protect him from any harm that could potentially be inflicted upon him, she knew she'd find herself facing the latter's wrath soon after. And once Akito has finally learned of the truth to Rin and Haru's secret relationship, this led her to scornfully convince Rin about how someone as damaged as herself doesn’t deserve to be with Haru, much less be loved by him. What's more, the latter's heinous remarks have also managed to trigger Rin's emotions some more, until she proceeded to throw her out of a second story window.[3][4] With the events of facing Akito's wrath, being hospitalized for an extended amount of time after being thrown out of a window, followed by being disowned from her parents, whom she originally thought that they loved her, the amount of trauma Rin has dealt with caused her former personality to undergo a major change, to the point where she becomes the aggressive, emotionally distant, jaded and abrasive young woman she is during her official debut. Despite coming off as sharp-tongued and wielding her words as a weapon, just to lash out at the world for the cruelty she experienced, Haru briefly mentions that Rin doesn’t hesitate to say things that will hurt other people, but she doesn’t say anything to protect herself either.[5] While Rin is considered to be a lone wolf who prefers to solely depend on herself and herself alone, this is very much shown when she becomes incredibly determined to find a way in breaking the curse without the help or interference from anyone, especially from someone like Tohru Honda. The latter of which whom Rin views as nothing more than a mere "outsider" that shouldn't be caught up in the affairs of the Sohma's. Behind the uncaring and aloof front that she displays, Rin is considered to be deeply loyal and passionate, as well as tremendously self-sacrificing, and selfless. characters.name: Isuzu's Father
characters.description: He is the estranged father ofIsuzu Sohma. He was initially seen to be a kind and loving father, always seen with a smile on his face, who loved his daughter deeply. However, when Isuzu questioned her parent’s love, his loving personality is revealed to have been an act, as he turned into an incredibly angry, selfish, and physically, verbally, and emotionally abused his daughter. In comparison to his wife, he is much quieter and leaves the talking to her, but is very cruel towards Isuzu and is not afraid to rub his hatred of her in her face. He also abandoned Isuzu without a second thought without thinking about the trauma he and his wife had inflicted on her.[1]

characters.name: Kagura Sohma
characters.description: Kagura is usually a sweet, polite, considerate, and friendly girl, but she is also very strong-willed, impulsive, and can become extremely violent, forceful, self-centered, and emotionally aggressive in a matter of seconds. This is most evident regarding her feelings for Kyo; when the target of her affections is present as her extremely passionate feelings are shown, they are also accompanied by actual physical violence as seen when she beats Kyo up. However, her behavior is mainly caused by emotional immaturity instead of malice; she just loses control of herself as her emotions well up and has little restraint at the very moment it happens. She charges at full force, like the Boar she embodies.[2] She has no sense of personal space and is very clingy with Kyo, but she has also been shown to hug other members likeYuki Sohma. She is affectionate and honest about her feelings for Kyo, but can become very aggressive when things don't go her way and when Kyo doesn't return her affections. Despite this, she is shown to genuinely be friendly and is noted to have a big-sister type of personality. She is also a very energetic person who wears her emotions on her sleeve. She becomes good friends withTohru Hondadespite viewing her as a romantic rival at first, and willingly gives up on Kyo when it's made clear that Tohru is the one he truly loves.[3] After being rejected by Kyo, Kagura realizes how self-centered she had been, and towards the end of the series, her personality mellows down to a gentler and more emotionally mature girl who has less violent outbursts and no problem to apologize if she thinks she has crossed a line.[4][5]Even though she is dishonest with her feelings, she genuinely wishes happiness for Kyo and Tohru.[6]
